Northern California:

Within 3 months, Boris counseled me effectively and helped me forge a path to resolution. This involved a settlement agreement being reached with the plaintiff, meeting and conferring to forge/amend this agreement in order to reach a resolution. Most importantly, the TRO was lifted and the CIVIL case dismissed by Memorial Day 2024.

The essential and indispensable element was Boris’ patient, unstirred, pragmatic counsel in the Criminal case. His counsel showed me the path to resolution in the CIVIL case was based on the Criminal case. He provided the necessary expertise to file multiple motions for continuance in the CIVIL case, thereby allowing the Criminal case to take its course and lead. He then appeared in SC for me as counsel and motioned the court for “Judicial Diversion”. Ultimately I was granted JD, with an emphasis on counseling. I received no jail time, did not face trial, no community service and no monetary fines. Boris is a fantastic, smart, and insightful attorney whose experience and education that resolved this case for me in the most ideal fashion. As I mentioned at the beginning, this originated with a dispute with neighbor. The only way to reach a settlement in the CIVIL matter was via Judicial Diversion in the Criminal matter. I would not have been able to do this without an experienced lawyer like Boris Bindman.

During these 3 months of 2024 (March --> May), I could not have had better LEGAL counsel. An arrest, being detained by LE, getting a notice on your door that you’ve been served with restraining orders; these are scary and disruptive things to everyday life. Boris Bindman is a highly effective Criminal defense attorney and I could not have asked for a better advocate or a smarter lawyer.

My family found Daniel Maloney on a list of elder law specialists put out by the Contra Costa County Bar.  Our 75 year old mom needed to retire and was facing a second change in housing due to real estate development. My family was trying to get an idea about what her options would be in the future for housing and care. Daniel met with us for an initial visit for a set fee, and we left with a more realistic sense of how to move forward with a retirement plan for our mom.

A few years later, we contacted Daniel again because our mom needed estate planning, and he proposed a reasonably priced package that included a will, living trust, and durable power of attorney documents. We met again and Daniel listened to everyone's concerns, spoke privately with my mom, engaged her as a client, and prepared the package of documents.  Within a year of this second meeting our mother was diagnosed with dementia, and we had to move her to an apartment near my brother for a year and then into residential care. By using the documents that Daniel prepared, we were able to manage her medical and financial affairs while her disease progressed, and fund the trust before my mom passed.  Because of Daniel's skill and experience working with elder law, we were able to move though some very difficult times and avoid many of the legal and financial log jams that can occur for families at the end of a parent's life.

Despite that, he took the time to thoroughly understand every detail and carefully guided me through the options.

At one point it even seemed like I exceeded the income thresholds for Chapter 7, but he skillfully used the six-month income average to show how my case truly fit within the guidelines. His knowledge of the law and attention to detail made all the difference.

What stood out even more was his encouragement throughout the process. During moments when I felt overwhelmed by the roller coaster of emotions, his words were always reassuring and gave me the strength to keep moving forward.

He also helped me separate from a very toxic job situation where I was dealing with chargebacks and financial pressure, and he made sure those issues were properly addressed and included in the bankruptcy. That level of advocacy meant a lot to me.
